# Project Instructions

## Tech Stack

| Category | Technology |
|----------|------------|
| Language | Markdown + YAML + Bash |
| Framework | Claude Code SDD meta-system |
| Entry point | SKILL.md per skill directory |
| Package manager | N/A (skill files, not code) |
| Testing | Manual validation via project-audit |
| Version control | Git |
| Build tool | install.sh (bash deploy) |
| Hooks runtime | Node.js |

### File types

| Type | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `SKILL.md` | Skill entry point — instructions Claude reads and executes |
| `config.yaml` | SDD openspec project configuration |
| `*.md` | Memory layer, plans, SDD artifacts |
| `*.yaml` | SDD config |
| `*.sh` | Bash scripts (sync, install) |
| `settings.json` | Claude Code user-level settings (MCP, permissions) |

### Skill categories

| Category | Count | Examples |
|----------|-------|---------|
| SDD phases | 8 | sdd-propose, sdd-spec, sdd-design, sdd-tasks, sdd-apply, sdd-verify, sdd-archive, sdd-explore |
| Meta-tools | 11 | project-setup, project-onboard, project-audit, project-analyze, project-fix, project-update, memory-init, memory-update, skill-creator, skill-add, codebase-teach |
| Tech — Frontend | 8 | react-19, nextjs-15, typescript, zustand-5, zod-4, tailwind-4, ai-sdk-5, react-native |
| Tech — Backend | 4 | django-drf, spring-boot-3, hexagonal-architecture-java, java-21 |
| Tech — Testing | 2 | playwright, pytest |
| Tech — Tooling | 6 | github-pr, jira-task, jira-epic, elixir-antipatterns, electron, smart-commit |
| Design Principles | 1 | solid-ddd |
| System Audits | 2 | claude-folder-audit, project-claude-organizer |

## Architecture

`agent-config` is the global configuration repository for Claude Code. It is deployed to `~/.claude/` via `install.sh`.

### Two-layer architecture

```
agent-config (repo)          ~/.claude/ (runtime)
      │                              │
      ├── CLAUDE.md    ──install──►  ├── CLAUDE.md
      ├── skills/      ──install──►  ├── skills/
      ├── settings.json ─install──►  ├── settings.json
      ├── hooks/       ──install──►  ├── hooks/
      ├── openspec/    ──install──►  ├── openspec/
      └── ai-context/  ──install──►  └── ai-context/
                            ◄──sync────  (memory/ only)
```

- `install.sh`: repo → `~/.claude/` (full deploy, one-directional)
- `sync.sh`: `~/.claude/memory/` → `repo/memory/` (memory capture only)

### Skill architecture

Every skill is a directory with a `SKILL.md` entry point. SKILL.md must declare a `format:` field:

- `procedural` (default): requires `## Process`
- `reference`: requires `## Patterns` or `## Examples`
- `anti-pattern`: requires `## Anti-patterns`

All formats require `**Triggers**` and `## Rules`.

### Inter-skill communication

Skills communicate via file artifacts only — no in-memory state passing. Key artifacts:

| Artifact | Producer | Consumer | Location |
|----------|---------|---------|----------|
| `audit-report.md` | project-audit | project-fix | `.claude/audit-report.md` |
| `openspec/changes/*/proposal.md` | sdd-propose | sdd-spec, sdd-design | `openspec/changes/<name>/` |
| `openspec/changes/*/tasks.md` | sdd-tasks | sdd-apply | `openspec/changes/<name>/` |
| `ai-context/*.md` | memory-init / memory-update | all skills | `ai-context/` |

### Key architectural decisions

- **Skills are directories, not files** — allows co-locating templates and sub-skills
- **Artifacts over in-memory state** — skills communicate via files, never via conversation context alone
- **install.sh is repo-authoritative** — all directories flow repo → `~/.claude/`; the only reverse direction is `sync.sh` (memory only)
- **solid-ddd is a universal design principles skill** — loaded unconditionally for all non-documentation code changes
- **sdd-apply enforces a 7-item Quality Gate** before task completion: SRP, abstraction appropriateness, DIP, domain model integrity, ISP, no scope creep, no over-engineering
- **Feedback sessions produce only proposals** — never SDD cycles; implementation happens in a separate session

## Conventions

### Language

ALL content MUST be in English. This includes SKILL.md files, config.yaml, ai-context/ files, openspec/ artifacts, and commit messages.

### Naming

| Element | Convention | Example |
|---------|-----------|---------|
| Skill directories | kebab-case | `project-audit/`, `sdd-propose/` |
| Skill entry point | UPPER | `SKILL.md` |
| SDD phase skills | `sdd-[phase]` prefix | `sdd-propose`, `sdd-apply` |
| Meta-tool skills | `project-[action]` prefix | `project-audit`, `project-fix` |
| Tech skills | `[tech]-[version]` | `react-19`, `nextjs-15` |
| SDD change names | kebab-case descriptive | `improve-project-audit` |
| Archived changes | `YYYY-MM-DD-[name]` | `2026-02-23-add-project-fix` |

### Git conventions

- Commit messages in English
- Conventional commits: `feat:`, `fix:`, `refactor:`, `docs:`, `chore:`
- Config changes workflow: edit in repo → `bash install.sh` → `git commit`
- Memory capture workflow: `bash sync.sh` → `git add memory/` → `git commit`
- Commit after each SDD phase (at minimum after apply and after archive)

### Error handling

- Bash: `set -e` + `|| true` for expected-failures
- SKILL.md: guard clauses with explicit "Stop here if…" statements
- Sub-agent return codes: `status: ok|warning|blocked|failed`

### PRD Convention

A Product Requirements Document is optional and precedes the SDD cycle for user-facing or product-level changes. Template: `docs/templates/prd-template.md`.

### ADR Convention

Architectural Decision Records use Nygard format. Location: `docs/adr/NNN-short-title.md`. Template: `docs/templates/adr-template.md`. The index `docs/adr/README.md` must be kept updated.

## SDD Development Workflow

The SDD (Specification-Driven Development) workflow structures every change through a sequence of phases, each producing a file artifact under `openspec/changes/<change-name>/`.

### Phase sequence

1. **Explore** (mandatory) — Investigates the codebase area relevant to the change. Produces `openspec/changes/<change-name>/exploration.md`.

2. **Propose** — Creates a change proposal with intent, scope, and technical approach. Produces `openspec/changes/<change-name>/proposal.md`. Reads `exploration.md` as input. Optionally creates `openspec/changes/<change-name>/prd.md` when a PRD template exists.

3. **Spec + Design** (run in parallel) — Two independent phases that can execute simultaneously:
   - **Spec**: Writes delta specifications with Given/When/Then scenarios. Produces `openspec/changes/<change-name>/specs/<domain>/spec.md`.
   - **Design**: Produces the technical design with architecture decisions and file change plan. Produces `openspec/changes/<change-name>/design.md`. May create `docs/adr/NNN-<slug>.md` for significant architectural decisions.

4. **Tasks** — Breaks down the design into an atomic, ordered, verifiable task plan. Produces `openspec/changes/<change-name>/tasks.md`. Requires both spec and design to be complete.

5. **Apply** — Implements the tasks in `tasks.md` phase by phase (3–4 tasks per batch). Enforces a Diagnosis Step before each task and a 7-item Quality Gate before marking a task complete. Applies a retry circuit breaker: after 3 failed attempts a task is marked `[BLOCKED]` and execution halts.

6. **Verify** — Verifies the implementation against specs, design, and task plan. Produces `openspec/changes/<change-name>/verify-report.md` with a `## Tool Execution` section containing command, exit code, and output summary. Criteria marked `[x]` require verifiable evidence.

7. **Archive** — Syncs delta specs to master specs and moves the change directory to `openspec/changes/archive/YYYY-MM-DD-<name>/`. Irreversible — requires user confirmation before proceeding.

### SDD artifact storage

```
openspec/
├── config.yaml
├── specs/
│   └── {domain}/spec.md
└── changes/
    ├── {change-name}/
    │   ├── exploration.md
    │   ├── proposal.md
    │   ├── prd.md (optional)
    │   ├── specs/{domain}/spec.md
    │   ├── design.md
    │   ├── tasks.md
    │   └── verify-report.md
    └── archive/
        └── YYYY-MM-DD-{name}/

docs/
└── adr/
    ├── README.md
    └── NNN-<slug>.md
```

### Fast-forward pattern

The fast-forward pattern runs the full pre-implementation sequence automatically: explore → propose → spec + design (parallel) → tasks. After this sequence completes, a summary is presented and the developer confirms whether to proceed to apply.

### Warning classification

During task planning, warnings are classified as:
- `MUST_RESOLVE` — blocks execution until the user provides an explicit answer (used for business rule decisions, external system ambiguities, data model choices)
- `ADVISORY` — logged but does not interrupt execution

### SDD compliance rules

- Every change to a skill requires going through the full SDD workflow at minimum
- Every archived change must have a `verify-report.md` with at least one checked criterion
- Feedback (observations, improvement ideas) produces only `proposal.md` files — implementation happens in a separate session

## Active SDD Coaching Instructions

When a developer mentions implementing a new feature, change, or fix:
- Proactively ask: "Would you like to follow the SDD workflow for this change?"
- If yes, guide them step by step: explore → propose → (spec + design in parallel) → tasks → apply → verify → archive
- Before starting apply, confirm that `proposal.md`, `design.md`, and `tasks.md` exist under `openspec/changes/<change-name>/`
- Remind the developer to run verify after implementation and archive once confirmed
- For breaking changes to core skills (orchestrator, SDD phases), recommend the full cycle over the fast-forward pattern
- When a user provides feedback or improvement ideas, guide them to create only `proposal.md` files — implementation should happen in a separate session

When reviewing or writing SKILL.md files:
- Ensure the `format:` field is declared in YAML frontmatter (valid values: `procedural` | `reference` | `anti-pattern`)
- Validate that the section contract for the declared format is satisfied
- All content must be in English

When suggesting commits:
- Use conventional commit format: `feat:`, `fix:`, `refactor:`, `docs:`, `chore:`
- After config changes, remind the developer to run `bash install.sh` before committing

## Working Principles

- Clean and readable code over "clever" code
- No over-engineering: only what is necessary for the current task
- No obvious comments; only where the logic is not self-evident
- Error handling at system boundaries (user input, external APIs)
- No speculative features or unnecessary backwards-compatibility hacks
- Tests as first-class citizens

## Project Memory Layer

Each project using this system has a memory layer in `ai-context/`:

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
| `stack.md` | Tech stack, versions, key tools |
| `architecture.md` | Architecture decisions and their rationale |
| `conventions.md` | Code conventions, naming, team patterns |
| `known-issues.md` | Known bugs, gotchas, current limitations |
| `changelog-ai.md` | Log of changes made by AI |
| `ai-context/features/*.md` | Feature-level domain knowledge per bounded context |

At the start of each session in a project with this structure, read the relevant `ai-context/` files. After completing significant work, update the corresponding files.

## Known Issues

- **CRLF line endings on Windows**: If `bash sync.sh` or `bash install.sh` emit `$'\r': command not found` errors, run `sed -i 's/\r$//' sync.sh install.sh` to fix. A `.gitattributes` file enforces LF for all scripts going forward.
- **sync.sh is memory-only**: `sync.sh` only copies `~/.claude/memory/` to `repo/memory/`. Never run it expecting to deploy or recover skill/config changes.
- **install.sh is one-directional**: Changes made directly in `~/.claude/` are lost when `install.sh` is next run. Always edit in the repo first.
- **GITHUB_TOKEN required for install.sh**: Define `GITHUB_TOKEN` as a system environment variable before running `install.sh`.
- **claude-folder-audit false positives**: Check 4 (orphaned artifact detection) may flag Claude Code internal runtime files as unexpected. These are false positives — ignore them.
- **ai-context/ marker awareness gap**: `/project-analyze` writes `[auto-updated]` markers in ai-context/ files; memory-update and project-update are not aware of these markers and could theoretically overlap. Theoretical risk — not observed in practice.
- **No automated tests for skills**: Skill validation is manual via project-audit. A future `/skill-test` meta-tool is planned.
